title: Evaluate Division
problem_statement: You are given an array of variable pairs equations and an array of real numbers values, where equations[i] = [Ai, Bi] and values[i] represent the equation Ai / Bi = values[i]. You are also given some queries, where queries[j] = [Cj, Dj] represents the jth query to evaluate Cj / Dj = ?. Return the answers to all queries. If a single answer cannot be determined, return -1.0. Note: The input is always valid. You may assume that evaluating the queries will not result in division by zero and that there is no contradiction.

examples.explanation: Given a / b = 1.5, b / c = 2.5, bc / cd = 5.0. queries are: a / c = 3.75, c / b = 0.4, bc / cd = 5.0, cd / bc = 0.2.

constraints: Ai, Bi, Cj, Dj consist of lower case English letters and digits.
intuition: Model as a graph where variables are nodes and equations are weighted edges. For each query, find the path product from numerator to denominator.

approaches.name: DFS with Path Product
approaches.description: Build undirected graph with weights. For each query, DFS from start to end, multiplying weights along the path.
approaches.why_not: BFS also works; DFS is recursive and straightforward for small graphs.
interview_commentary: Graph is undirected with reciprocal weights. DFS accumulates product along path.
Use visited set to avoid cycles. If no path, return -1.
Handles transitive relations through graph connectivity.

approaches.name: BFS with Path Product
approaches.description: Use BFS with queue storing (node, current_product). Stop when reaching target.
approaches.why_not: DFS is more common, but BFS avoids recursion stack for deep graphs.
interview_commentary: BFS level-order traversal, updating product at each step.
Use a map for current product to each node.
Stops when target is found or queue empty.

interview_qa.a: Because a/b = x implies b/a = 1/x, so bidirectional.

interview_qa.q: What if query has unknown variable?

interview_qa.a: Should be 1.0, but if a not in equations, -1.
Union-Find doesn't handle multiplicative weights easily.
